atoms.content: Overfitting happens when a model learns the training data too perfectly, memorizing the noise and failing entirely to generalize to new, unseen data.
Underfitting happens when a model is too simple to capture the underlying patterns in the data, performing poorly on both training and test sets.
Underfitting is studying for 5 minutes and failing the test. Overfitting is memorizing the practice test answers but failing when the real test changes the numbers.
Generalization is the ultimate goal of ML: a model's ability to adapt properly to new, previously unseen data drawn from the same distribution.

1. Overfitting: High train accuracy, low test accuracy.
2. Underfitting: Low train accuracy, low test accuracy.
3. Goal: The 'Goldilocks' zone.
If your model gets 99% accuracy on training data but only 50% on test data, what is happening? (Answer: Overfitting).
